_doc: CHARACTERS for Ednar's Cauldron. See CHARACTER_AUTHORING_GUIDE.md for full template, dialogue advice, and tuning ranges. Each character id (the lowercase key like 'mildred') is what rounds.json references when assigning customers to a round. Trait field references traits.json by id, or null for no trait. To add a new character: copy the _template_for_new_characters block at the bottom, paste it into the 'characters' object, change values.
_format: Plain JSON. Lines starting with an underscore (_doc, _note) are comments for humans — the game ignores them. Strings use double quotes. Numbers (hp, patience, etc.) have no quotes. The trait field is either a string (the trait id) or null.
_organization: Characters below are grouped by difficulty (1=easiest, 5=boss-tier). Round builder uses 'difficulty' and 'time_of_day' to slot them. Bosses (difficulty 5) only spawn at night.

_combat_fields_doc._note: Session 4 turn-based combat. EVERY turn, each customer in queue attacks. The four combat fields define how:
_combat_fields_doc.active_attack: Damage the customer deals when they're at the front of the line (queue[0], being brewed for). Should be larger than waiting_attack.
_combat_fields_doc.waiting_attack: Damage the customer deals while waiting in line behind the active customer. Smaller — represents jeering, fidgeting, presence-pressure. Most customers: 1.
_combat_fields_doc.active_patience_tick: How fast their patience drops per turn while active. Default 1. Skittish trait makes this 2.
_combat_fields_doc.waiting_patience_tick: How fast their patience drops per turn while waiting. Default 1.
_combat_fields_doc.expire_damage: ONE-TIME damage when patience hits 0 and they storm out. Then they're removed from the queue.
_combat_fields_doc._tuning_rule: Sum of all queue active_attack + waiting_attack values per turn should be SURVIVABLE if the player just zero-brews for 4-5 turns (i.e. composure 30 / total per turn >= 4). Bosses can break this rule because the round is one customer.

mildred._note: DIFFICULTY 1 / Morning. Tutorial customer. HP 10, gentlest attacks (1/1). Player can defeat in 1-2 turns easily. If Day 1 morning feels too easy, raise HP to 12-14.
mildred.name: Mildred Honeycomb
mildred.title: Anxious Farmwife
mildred.order_dialogue: Oh dear, please be quick — my chickens have been at the cabbages again...
mildred.tick_dialogue: She fidgets, glancing toward the door.
mildred.expire_dialogue: Oh, forget it! I'll be back tomorrow.
mildred.defeat_dialogue: Oh, bless you, brewer. Bless you!

tomik._note: DIFFICULTY 1 / Morning. The 'no-pressure' customer. Long patience (7), low expire damage (3). His role is to be the customer you can ignore for a turn while you handle someone urgent.
tomik.name: Tomik Cooper
tomik.title: Sleepy Apprentice
tomik.tick_dialogue: Tomik yawns and leans on the counter.
tomik.defeat_dialogue: Oh! Oh, that's lovely. Thank you, brewer.

greta._note: DIFFICULTY 1-2 / Morning or Afternoon. The friendly customer. Inspiring trait gives +1 to all dice rolls while she waits. This is a reward — players will learn to keep her in queue longer for the buff. Tune carefully.
greta.name: Greta Marshlow
greta.title: Cheerful Villager
greta.order_dialogue: Hi there, friend! Got time for one more today?

pemberton._note: DIFFICULTY 2 / Afternoon. The mild grumpy merchant. Active attack 2 — first customer who hits harder than baseline. Teaches the player that not all customers are equal.
pemberton.name: Pemberton Quill
pemberton.title: Travelling Merchant
pemberton.order_dialogue: I haven't got all day, brewer. Wagon's leaving at noon.
pemberton.tick_dialogue: Pemberton taps his coin purse impatiently.
pemberton.expire_dialogue: Bah! Time wasted! I'll buy in the next town.

sister_halla._note: DIFFICULTY 2 / Morning or Afternoon. The 'patient and gentle' customer. Pious trait does nothing mechanically (by design) — flavor only. Long patience (8), low pressure. Use her to teach the player that a trait isn't always a threat.
sister_halla.name: Sister Halla
sister_halla.title: Wandering Sister
sister_halla.order_dialogue: ...take your time, brewer. The Light is patient.
sister_halla.tick_dialogue: Sister Halla murmurs a quiet prayer.
sister_halla.defeat_dialogue: May the Light bless your craft.

ardo._note: DIFFICULTY 2 / Morning or Afternoon. Skittish trait — active_patience_tick is 2 (drains twice as fast while at the front). Low attack but ticks toward expire fast. Pair with a long-patience customer like Sister Halla in the same round for clear contrast.
ardo.name: Ardo Quill
ardo.title: Nervous Scholar
ardo.order_dialogue: Th-three pages overdue! Please, please be quick!
ardo.expire_dialogue: Oh no oh no oh — *runs out without paying*
ardo.defeat_dialogue: Oh! Oh thank goodness, thank you!

wendelina._note: DIFFICULTY 3 / Afternoon or Evening. Hedge witch. Active attack 3 — first big hitter. No trait but high stats. Use her as 'serious customer who demands focus.'
wendelina.name: Wendelina Rookpool
wendelina.title: Hedge Witch
wendelina.order_dialogue: Brew well, brewer. I'll know if you don't.
wendelina.expire_dialogue: Pathetic. Don't speak to me again.

bram._note: DIFFICULTY 3 / Afternoon or Evening. Loud trait (currently STUBBED — declared but doesn't fire yet). Standard attack values for now.
bram.name: Bram the Bard
bram.title: Travelling Lutist
bram.order_dialogue: Brew me something with ZEST, master alchemist!
bram.expire_dialogue: *plays a sad chord and wanders off*
bram.defeat_dialogue: Aha! Now THAT is a potion! *fanfare*

crispin._note: DIFFICULTY 3 / Afternoon or Evening. Intimidating trait — your brew targets are +2 while he waits. Big active attack (3). Players who let him sit in queue feel the pressure compound.
crispin.name: Lord Crispin Vorne
crispin.title: Petty Noble
crispin.order_dialogue: I assume you can manage something... acceptable?
crispin.tick_dialogue: Crispin sneers and inspects his cuff.
crispin.expire_dialogue: Disgraceful. I shall write the council.

hexa_mott._note: DIFFICULTY 4 / Evening. Hexer trait (STUBBED). Hard active attack (3) plus high HP. Watch tuning — too high makes her oppressive.
hexa_mott.name: Hexa Mott
hexa_mott.title: Murky Witch
hexa_mott.tick_dialogue: Hexa's fingers twitch in odd patterns.
hexa_mott.expire_dialogue: Pity. *vanishes in a puff of green*
hexa_mott.defeat_dialogue: Mmm. I'll remember this. (Is that a threat?)

ironhilde._note: DIFFICULTY 4 / Evening. Draining trait bleeds 1 composure per turn. Her threat is attritional — she's a 'must serve quickly' customer that punishes triage delays. Pair with non-draining hard customers in the same round so the player has to choose who to tackle first.
ironhilde.name: Captain Ironhilde
ironhilde.title: Battle-Weary Knight
ironhilde.order_dialogue: Make it strong. I haven't slept in three days.
ironhilde.tick_dialogue: Her armor creaks as she shifts.
ironhilde.expire_dialogue: *coughs blood, walks out without a word*
ironhilde.defeat_dialogue: You have a soldier's gratitude, brewer.

grimdrek._note: DIFFICULTY 5 / NIGHT BOSS. The original Volatile demon. Overbrewing him triggers death-throes BEFORE your shield/heal applies. Big HP (32), big active attack (4). Single customer in night round so attack values can be higher.
grimdrek.name: Grimdrek the Volatile
grimdrek.title: Hellsworn Merchant
grimdrek.expire_dialogue: *explodes into the shop with a roar*

carmilla._note: DIFFICULTY 5 / NIGHT BOSS. Hexer trait (STUBBED) plus heavy active attack (4). HP 30 means you can't take your time.
carmilla.name: Lady Carmilla Veil
carmilla.title: Vampire Countess
carmilla.order_dialogue: I'm... particular, darling. Try to keep up.
carmilla.defeat_dialogue: Mmm. You have... potential. I'll be back.

royal_envoy._note: DIFFICULTY 5 / NIGHT BOSS. The 'imminent disaster' boss — Intimidating trait pushes targets +2, big HP (36), short patience (4), HUGE expire damage (14), heavy active attack (5). Can end a run if mishandled.
royal_envoy.name: The Royal Envoy
royal_envoy.title: On Crown Business
royal_envoy.order_dialogue: By order of the Crown — make it perfect.
royal_envoy.tick_dialogue: He taps the royal seal pointedly.
royal_envoy.expire_dialogue: The Crown will hear of this. *storms out*
royal_envoy.defeat_dialogue: Acceptable work. The Crown thanks you.

_template_for_new_characters._note: TEMPLATE — Copy the block below, rename the key (e.g. 'jorek_smith'), fill in values, and move it into the 'characters' object above. Tuning rules of thumb: difficulty 1 = HP 8-12 / patience 7-8 / active_attack 1 / waiting_attack 1 / expire 3-4. Difficulty 2 = HP 12-16 / patience 5-7 / active_attack 1-2 / waiting_attack 1 / expire 5-6. Difficulty 3 = HP 16-20 / patience 4-5 / active_attack 2-3 / waiting_attack 1 / expire 6-7. Difficulty 4 = HP 20-26 / patience 4-5 / active_attack 3 / waiting_attack 1-2 / expire 7-9. Difficulty 5 (BOSS) = HP 28-40 / patience 4-6 / active_attack 4-5 / waiting_attack 2 / expire 12+. The 'trait' field is either a trait id from traits.json (in quotes) or null (no quotes). Bosses always have time_of_day ['night'] only.

your_character_id.name: Display Name
your_character_id.title: Job or Vibe
your_character_id.order_dialogue: What they say when they walk in.
your_character_id.tick_dialogue: Flavor for what they do each turn.
your_character_id.expire_dialogue: What they say when they storm off.
